finding: A strong classical baseline remained highly competitive on this task.
why_it_matters: Shows that simpler sparse-text models can remain highly effective.

finding: The neutral class was the most difficult class to classify consistently.
why_it_matters: Indicates that ambiguous or weakly polar language remains challenging.

finding: Grouped splitting reduced duplicate-text leakage risk.
DistilBERT provided a context-aware comparison under the same split policy.
why_it_matters: Makes the classical-versus-transformer comparison more methodologically fair.

finding: Performance should be interpreted in light of rating-derived labels rather than manual sentiment labels.
why_it_matters: Clarifies the scope and limitations of the prediction task.
